checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Hugging Face Transformers is an open-source Python library providing state-of-the-art pretrained models for natural language processing, computer vision, audio, and multimodal tasks for both inference and training.

The software is used to:

Coordinate cross-model collaboration among multiple large language models
Implement contextual bandit and baseline routing algorithms for the MultiWeave framework
Provide the foundation models (LLMs) that will be orchestrated in the routing experiments
Enable logging and evaluation of model performance

Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
The NVIDIA vLLM NGC container is an optimized Docker image containing vLLM, a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for Large Language Models with GPU acceleration.

vLLM is used for high-throughput inference of multiple LLMs on GH200 GPUs as part of the MultiWeave framework's bandit-style routing experiments and model orchestration.

Aligns with approved research topic Datascience & engineering

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MedQA contains multiple-choice questions from professional medical board exams (USMLE in US, medical licensing exams in Mainland China and Taiwan) with 12,723 English, 34,251 simplified Chinese, and 14,123 traditional Chinese questions, along with medical textbook corpora for knowledge retrieval.

Based on the proposal, MedQA is used for Objective 1 (building a scalable routing framework across multiple scenarios), Objective 2 (evaluating bandit-style routing algorithms), and Objective 3 (implementing and comparing non-bandit baselines), specifically as one of the public benchmarks for evaluating multi-model orchestration and collaboration approaches in the medical domain.

Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and Engineering

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MedMCQA contains over 194,000 multiple-choice questions from Indian medical entrance exams (AIIMS & NEET PG, years 1991-present), covering 2,400 healthcare topics across 21 medical subjects, with each question including answer options, correct answer, and expert explanations.

Based on the proposal, MedMCQA ("medmcqa") is explicitly listed as one of the public benchmarks for evaluating multi-model orchestration, supporting Objective 1 (scalable routing framework evaluation), Objective 2 (bandit-style routing algorithm testing), and Objective 3 (baseline comparison), specifically for validating routing and collaboration approaches across medical domain question-answering scenarios.


Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and engineering

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
The dataset contains 1,273 multiple-choice questions from the United States Medical Licensing Examination (USMLE) test split, each with four answer options, correct answer index, USMLE step classification (step1 or step2&3), and MetaMap phrase annotations.

Based on the proposal, MedQA-USMLE is listed as one of the public benchmarks supporting Objectives 1-3: building the scalable routing framework, implementing/evaluating bandit-style routing algorithms, and comparing non-bandit baselines for medical domain question-answering.

Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and engineering

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MMLU is a massive multitask test consisting of multiple-choice questions from various branches of knowledge spanning 57 tasks including elementary mathematics, US history, computer science, law, and covering subjects in the humanities, social sciences, hard sciences, and other areas.

Based on the proposal, MMLU is listed as one of the public benchmarks supporting Objectives 1-3: building the scalable routing framework, implementing/evaluating bandit-style routing algorithms, and comparing non-bandit baselines for multi-domain question-answering evaluation.



Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and engineering

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MultiWeave is a cross-model collaboration framework that orchestrates multiple foundation AI models using contextual bandit algorithms to learn which model(s) to query for a given input, balancing accuracy, latency, and compute cost trade-offs rather than relying on a single model or static ensembles.

MultiWeave directly supports all five proposal objectives: (1) building a scalable routing framework on GH200 GPUs, (2) implementing/evaluating bandit-style routing algorithms, (3) implementing non-bandit baselines for comparison, (4) quantifying quality-compute trade-offs, and (5) releasing reproducible code and evaluation tools.

It is an in-house code developed by the PI's group. 

This is a pipeline rather than a model. 

Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and engineering.

FLOPS = 6 x N x D = 6 x 8 x 10^9 x 1 x 10^9 = 4.8 x 10^19

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
DeepSeekMath-7B-Instruct is a mathematically specialized language model designed for step-by-step mathematical reasoning, solving math problems using chain-of-thought prompting, and providing structured answers to quantitative questions in English and Chinese.

Included as a default model in /ibex/project/c2320/AIMCR/65567-amicr/multiweave_code_20260121_1120/code/comed_run_multi.py


Aligns with the approved research topic Datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes:
DeepSeekMath is initialized with DeepSeek-Coder-v1.5 7B and continues pre-training on math-related tokens sourced from Common Crawl, together with natural language and code data for 500B tokens.

Pre-training data composition:

120B math-related tokens sourced from Common Crawl arXiv (DeepSeekMath Corpus)

4% AlgebraicStack, 10% arXiv texts, 20% GitHub code, 10% natural language (EN/ZH) Emergent Mind

Total continued pre-training: 500 billion tokens

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes:
DeepSeekMath-Instruct 7B was fine-tuned on 776K training examples covering:

Mathematical problem-solving with chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning

Program-of-thought (PoT) reasoning

Tool-integrated reasoning

GSM8K and MATH benchmark problems with tool-integrated solutions

English and Chinese mathematical problems across varying complexity levels

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
The indication of number of parameters for bandit network is 7B. 
The indication of number of training tokens is 500B. 
The estimated FLOPs on ShaheenIII GPUP to train a model from scratch is: 6 x N x D= 6 x 7x 10^9 x 500 x 10^9 = 2.1 x 10^22 FLOPS

It is less than the threshold of 10^27.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes: o risk in LC2.7 check. The model is not intended for pre-training, therefore, reducing LC 2.7 risk to low. Since LC2.7 is no-risk and 2.5 is low, skipping the inspection.

checks.notes:
Qwen License (custom, based on Tongyi Qianwen License):
Free to use, reproduce, modify, and distribute for commercial and non-commercial purposes.
Must include copy of license and attribution notices with distributions. 
Modified files must carry prominent modification notices. 
If commercial use exceeds 100 million monthly active users, must request additional license from Alibaba. 
Cannot use model outputs to improve other LLMs (except Qwen derivatives). 
Must comply with export controls and applicable laws. 
You own derivative works you create. 
No trademark license granted except for required notices.

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes: pre-trained from scratch on 18 trillion tokens from proprietary Alibaba datasets covering 29+ languages, with emphasis on knowledge, coding, and mathematics.

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes: This model, as is, has not been fine-tuned on any downstream task, according to the model card from HuggingFace.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
The indication of number of parameters is 7B. 
The indication of number of training tokens is 18T. 
The estimated FLOPs = 6 × 7 × 10^9 × 18 × 10^12 = 7.56× 10^23 FLOPs.
It is less than the threshold of 10^27.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes: No risk in LC2.7 check. The model is not intended for pre-training, therefore, reducing LC 2.7 risk to low. Since LC2.7 is no-risk and 2.5 is low, skipping inspection.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
A 7 billion-parameter instruction-tuned generative language model designed to follow natural language instructions and generate text across conversational and completion tasks.

Aligns with approved research topic Datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms like “military,” “weapons,” or “nuclear” in the model documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7.

checks.name: Source / Provenance & D5+M Affiliation Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes:
The model is owned and maintained by Mistral AI (https://mistral.ai/about), a French AI company specializing in open and performant large language models. Entity from a Non-D5 country.

Source: https://huggingface.co/mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes:
License: Apache 2.0

You are free to use the model for any purpose, including commercial applications under the Apache 2.0 license.

You may modify and distribute original or derived models.

You must include copyright notices and the full license text when redistributing.

No warranty or liability is provided by the licensors (as per Apache 2.0 terms).

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes: The base Mistral-7B-Instruct was fine-tuned from Mistral-7B-v0.2 using publicly available conversational and instruction datasets (exact corpus not fully documented), and broadly the underlying base models of this family are in the tens-to-hundreds of billions of tokens range typical for open transformer LLMs.

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes: It was fine-tuned for instruction following and conversational tasks, making it suitable for chat, question answering, and general text generation when prompted with [INST]…[/INST]-formatted instructions.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
The indication of number of parameters for bandit network is 7B. 
The estimation of number of training tokens is 2T (No official numbers found). 
The estimated FLOPs on ShaheenIII GPUP to train a model from scratch is: 6 x N x D= 6 x 7x 10^9 x 2x 10^12 = 8.4 x 10^22 FLOPS
It is less than the threshold of 10^27.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes: No risk in LC2.7 check, skipping inspection.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
An English text embedding model that converts input text into 1024-dimensional vectors for semantic similarity, retrieval, clustering, or classification tasks with support for up to 8192 tokens of context.

Aligns with approved research topic Datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms (e.g., “military”, “weapons”, “nuclear”) in the model documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7.

checks.name: Source / Provenance & D5+M Affiliation Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes:
The model is developed and maintained by the Institute for Intelligent Computing, Alibaba Group, part of the larger Chinese tech company Alibaba. Entity is from a D5 country (China).

Source: https://huggingface.co/Alibaba-NLP/gte-large-en-v1.5

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes:
License: Apache-2.0

Free for commercial or research use with nearly unrestricted reuse rights under the Apache License 2.0 (permissive open-source).

You may modify and distribute the model and derivative works.

Redistributions must include copyright & license text.

Patent rights are granted under the license, but any patent litigation terminates those rights.

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes: Training included masked language modeling (C4-en), weak-supervised contrastive pre-training, and supervised contrastive fine-tuning on GTE-specific data; the exact token count is not published, but the dataset size listed is ~10.4 billion tokens from allenai/c4 in the Hugging Face dataset viewer.

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes: It’s pre-trained/contrastively tuned for general text representation (embeddings), optimized for tasks like semantic similarity, text retrieval, clustering, and reranking.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
The indication of number of parameters for bandit network is 0.43B. 
The indication of number of training tokens is ~10–15 B tokens. 
The estimated FLOPs on ShaheenIII GPUP to train a model from scratch is: 6 x N x D= 6 x 0.43x 10^9 x 15 x 10^9 = 3.9 x 10^19 FLOPS
It is less than the threshold of 10^27.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes: No risk in LC2.7 check, skipping inspection.

observations: - MultiWeave CoMed is not a model but a pipeline used to route the parts of a query to appropriate LLMs based on suitability to optimize the response performance. The PI has provided the source code in  /ibex/project/c2320/AIMCR/65567-amicr/. Although the PI has not requested any models either in the project proposal or in the additional information they have provided, their code lists some models by default. The AIMCR has added these models in the review.
